title: CAMbank: Environmental Swabbing in Flight (v1)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bahgm","text":"This protocol details the standardized collection of environmental microbial samples from spacecraft interiors during flight, using sterile swabs and nucleic acid preservative to enable downstream microbial community analyses. During the SpaceX Inspiration4 mission, environmental swabs were collected from predetermined high-contact and representative surfaces inside the Dragon capsule on Flight Days 1 and 2, as well as from a crew training capsule pre-flight. Ten locations were sampled at each timepoint, including control surfaces, instrument panels, viewing ports, waste locker lid, hatch handles, and crew seat headrests. For each site, sterile DNA/RNA swabs were pre-moistened with nuclease-free water (in-flight: hydrogen-infused water) and applied with firm pressure for 30 seconds, ensuring maximum surface coverage. Swabs were then placed in 2D matrix tubes containing DNA/RNA Shield preservative and stored at ambient temperature during flight. After return to Earth, samples were transferred to −80°C storage in the Cornell Aerospace Medicine Biobank (CAMbank). This method is compatible with metagenomic, metatranscriptomic, and targeted qPCR assays to characterize microbial composition, diversity, and potential pathogenicity in the spacecraft environment. The approach supports the Space Omics and Medical Atlas (SOMA) initiative’s objective of developing standardized environmental biospecimen protocols to enable cross-mission comparisons and track microbe–crew–habitat interactions over time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bk8q2","text":"Sterile swabs (Isohelix DNA/RNA Swabs) (in-flight)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dvb0r","text":"Pre-labeled 2D matrix tubes with Zymo DNA/RNA Shield (400 µL per tube/sample) (in-flight)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1hf22","text":"Sterile nitrile gloves (in-flight)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4rd39","text":"70% Ethanol in a spray bottle (ground)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ggqo","text":"VWR KimWipes (ground)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2k8f6","text":"Cryogenic Freezer (ground)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6m9d8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Locate all flight materials in the space capsule.
Put on nitrile gloves to ensure sterile handling of the samples.

Swab pre-selected areas of the space capsule cabin by matching the IDs on the tubes to the areas of the capsule. Wet each swab with sterile water. Swab each area for 30 seconds. Use both sides of the swab. Open the 2D matrix tube and deposit the swab in the Zymo DNA/RNA Shield preservative.
Return tubes to ambient cabin storage.
After landing, transfer samples to science team.
Science team performs an inventory and inspection of the samples.
Science team stores samples at +4°C (if processing imminently) or at -80°C (for long-term storage).
Tube exteriors are sterlized with 70% EtOH and a KimWipe.
creator: Eliah G Overbey
